channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Peter Molyneux's 'God Game' Masters of Albion Arrives in Early Access April 22
item.content:encoded:
Peter Molyneux and 22cans are finally ready to put Masters of Albion out there for all to play. The 'god game' launches into Steam Early Access on April 22, 2026, and builds on Molyneux vision of a living world controlled by you. 

If you've been out of the loop on Masters of Albion, the latest Molyneux title is an attempt to distill everything he built across Black & White, Dungeon Keeper, and the Fable series into a single modern package. The game loop cycles with having you build and manage a fully customizable town and work through the game's narrative. As night creeps in, creatures descend on that same town and you defend it in real-time combat, using whatever powers you've assembled to keep things standing until morning. It's a deliberately old-school loop dressed up in contemporary design.

The team behind it carries serious pedigree. Alongside Molyneux, 22cans brought in Fable composer Russell Shaw, LittleBigPlanet and Media Molecule co-founder Mark Healey, artist Kareem Ettouney, and writer Iain Wright, the same crew whose fingerprints are on a lot of the Lionhead catalog that fans of the era still talk about.

We covered the Masters of Albion announcement back in 2024, when Molyneux openly discussed using profits from his NFT title Legacy to fund the project and freely acknowledged his history of overpromising. The candor was appreciated, if nothing else. With his new game on the horizon, we can only hope it delivers on everything the trailers have shown - and more. 

Players who buy and play within the first week of Early Access will receive a Founders Paint Pack, an exclusive set of in-game colors for early supporters. The game is available now on Steam priced at $24.99 with a 10% early adopter discount at launch.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/peter-molyneuxs-god-game-masters-of-albion-arrives-in-early-access-april-22-2000137839
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Torchlight: Infinite SS12 Lunaria Is Live: New Hero, New Farming Loop, and Two Permanent Systems
item.content:encoded:
Torchlight: Infinite SS12 Lunaria is now live on PC and mobile, and XD Games is wasting no time making the game's third anniversary count. We recently detailed the new season, which brings the Lunaria farming loop, a new hero, and a pair of permanent additions that are sticking around well past the season's end.

The seasonal mechanic centers on Lunar Stone Statues scattered across Netherrealm. Players wielding Lunar Rings awaken these statues to summon Lunari creatures, and defeating them drops both loot and energy to keep the chain going. Randomly triggered Lunar Omens and Rhapsodies mix things up mid-run, with effects ranging from spawning a golden statue loaded with better loot to resetting all Lunari creatures back into statues.

New hero Scent Weaver Sage is available now as well. She's an elixir-based fighter who tweaks her recipes mid-combat and can pick up defensive stats not typically found on gear. Her arrival also brings a full potion system overhaul, rebranded as Elixir Skills, which now run on charges rather than mana.

On the permanent side, Creation Engines are live across the map. Clear the guarding enemies, and nearby Creation Crystals convert into high-end loot, with Netherrealm Talent investment unlocking stronger options. The Modularization system is also in, letting players summon a copy of a recently defeated boss to fight alongside them, with 20 bosses available at launch.

For those looking to get a full rundown before they jump in, you can check out the full patch notes on the official site.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/torchlight-infinite-ss12-lunaria-is-live-new-hero-new-farming-loop-and-two-permanent-systems-2000137838
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
